com.mentor.chs.api.report

Interface IXObjectComparator

  • All Known Subinterfaces:
    IXAttributeComparator

    public interface IXObjectComparator
    Object comparators allow to specify the objects that are to be considered for change and to build the changes, additions and deletions. Three types of modifications can be built, which are identified as
    change - the object has a matching component in the reference design but has a change with respect to this comparator
    addition - the object has no matching component in reference design and
    deletion - the object has no matching component in current design.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      java.util.Set<IXChangedObject> buildAdditions(java.util.Set<IXObject> objects, IXDesignComparisonContext ctx)
      Takes the set of objects that have been added to the source design If the comparator is interested in any of them then they need to be added to the overall list
      java.util.Set<IXChangedObject> buildChanges(java.util.Map<IXObject,IXObject> objects, IXDesignComparisonContext ctx)
      Take a Map of matching objects to compare and test
      java.util.Set<IXChangedObject> buildDeletions(java.util.Set<IXObject> objects, IXDesignComparisonContext ctx)
      Takes the set of objects that have been removed from the source design If the comparator is interested in any of them then they need to be added to the overall list
      boolean interestedInObject(IXObject object)
      When the list of objects for a design is being gathered the comparaotrs are asked for their interest in an object If no comparator is interested then that object is excluded from the comparison

    • Method Detail

      • interestedInObject

        boolean interestedInObject(IXObject object)
        When the list of objects for a design is being gathered the comparaotrs are asked for their interest in an object If no comparator is interested then that object is excluded from the comparison
        Parameters:
        object - the object to check for interest
        Returns:
        true if you want this object to be included in the comparison in any way

      • buildChanges

        java.util.Set<IXChangedObject> buildChanges(java.util.Map<IXObject,IXObject> objects,
                                            IXDesignComparisonContext ctx)
        Take a Map of matching objects to compare and test
        Parameters:
        objects - the Map of matching objects
        Returns:
        If any of the object pairs is different according to this comparator then a new ChangedObject must be created and added to the return set

      • buildAdditions

        java.util.Set<IXChangedObject> buildAdditions(java.util.Set<IXObject> objects,
                                              IXDesignComparisonContext ctx)
        Takes the set of objects that have been added to the source design If the comparator is interested in any of them then they need to be added to the overall list
        Parameters:
        objects - the Set of objects added to the diagram
        Returns:
        the Set of objects the comparator is interested in being added

      • buildDeletions

        java.util.Set<IXChangedObject> buildDeletions(java.util.Set<IXObject> objects,
                                              IXDesignComparisonContext ctx)
        Takes the set of objects that have been removed from the source design If the comparator is interested in any of them then they need to be added to the overall list
        Parameters:
        objects - the Set of objects removed from the diagram
        Returns:
        the Set of objects the comparator is interested in
